name: ExtensionAdverseEvent_Outcome
title: R5: Type of outcome from the adverse event
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AdverseEvent.outcome` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AdverseEvent.outcome` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AdverseEvent.outcome` 0..* `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `AdverseEvent.outcome` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `AdverseEvent.outcome` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AdverseEvent.outcome` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `AdverseEvent.outcome` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AdverseEvent.outcome` as `RelatedTo`.

element.short: R5: Type of outcome from the adverse event
element.comment:
Element `AdverseEvent.outcome` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `AdverseEvent.outcome` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AdverseEvent.outcome` as `RelatedTo`.

element.short: Type of outcome from the adverse event
element.definition: Describes the type of outcome from the adverse event, such as resolved, recovering, ongoing, resolved-with-sequelae, or fatal.
